Paragraph

Any Cauchy sequence \(\{ c_{k}\}\) in \(C[a, b]\) under the \(L^{p}[a, b]\)-norm has a subsequence, still denoted as \(\{ c_{k}\}\text{,}\) such that
\begin{equation*} \Vert c_{k} -c_{k+1}\Vert_{L^{p}[a, b]}\le \epsilon_{k}^{2}, \end{equation*}
where \(\epsilon_{k} > 0\) are such that \(\sum_{k} \epsilon_{k}\) converges.
in-context
